Home.title: Welcome to Smiles by Dr Craig Hadgis Orthodontist

paragraphs.text: Thank you for visiting Smiles by Dr Craig Hadgis Orthodontist. Our practice is dedicated to providing the highest-quality pediatric and orthodontic care in a friendly, family-focused environment. We combine personalized care with advanced digital technology — including 3D imaging, digital treatment planning, and modern appliance systems — so every treatment is precise, efficient, and comfortable. We welcome patients of all ages from St. Augustine, St. Johns, and Jacksonville and offer complimentary growth-and-development exams, clear aligner options, custom digital braces, and flexible financing to make great smiles accessible for your family.

items.title: No Referral Needed
items.text: Book a free evaluation anytime — no referral required for your first visit.

items.title: Advanced Digital Care
items.text: 3D imaging, AI-assisted planning, and custom systems like KL|Owen, Insignia, and LightForce for precise results.

items.title: Comfortable Treatments
items.text: Options like Damon tie-less brackets and clear aligners give faster, more comfortable care with fewer visits.

items.title: Transparent Financing
items.text: Clear financing, insurance help, online account access, and a financial calculator to plan payments.

paragraphs.text: Dr. Hadgis is board-certified and leads the practice with a mission to provide the highest quality pediatric and orthodontic care in a pleasant, patient-focused manner. He keeps the practice current with advanced technologies such as 3D scanning, robotics, AI-assisted treatment planning, and custom digital appliance systems to deliver personalized, precise care.

image.alt: Dr. Craig Hadgis, orthodontist at Smiles by Dr Craig Hadgis

paragraphs.text: From removable clear aligners to fully customized 3D-printed braces, we offer modern treatment options tailored to each patient.

image.alt: What sets us apart at Dr. Craig Hadgis Orthodontics

paragraphs.text: Our mission is to provide each and every patient with the highest quality, most affordable orthodontic services available anywhere in a manner that makes the entire experience a pleasure. From the very first visit to the beautiful new smile we help create, you can be sure you will receive only the best. This is our commitment to you. Expect nothing less than caring, honest, and affordable orthodontic care.

Dr. Hadgis prides himself on providing an excellent patient experience while remaining respectful of appointment times. He and the team design efficient, cost-conscious treatment plans tailored to St. Augustine and St. Johns families so care is predictable and comfortable.

The Value of a Smile
A simple curve of courage and light,
It opens doors and starts the day right.
We are honored to care for your family and look forward to guiding you through every step of treatment with compassion and clear communication. — Dr. Craig Hadgis and the team

Dr. Craig Hadgis is an orthodontic specialist and a Diplomate of the American Board of Orthodontics. He brings more than a decade of experience delivering customized, digitally planned orthodontic care with an emphasis on predictable results and a comfortable patient experience.
Board-certified and a Certified Platinum Invisalign Provider, Dr. Hadgis treats children, teens, and adults using clear aligners and contemporary fixed appliance options to meet each patient's goals.

image.alt: Dr. Craig Hadgis in treatment room

paragraphs.text: Dr. Hadgis earned a B.S. in Industrial Engineering and an M.S. in Biomedical Engineering from The University of Michigan, followed by a D.D.S. from the University of Detroit Mercy and post-DDS advanced education in general dentistry. He completed his Orthodontic Specialty Certification at Jacksonville University.

The Hadgis family has deep ties to the St. Augustine area. Dr. Hadgis is married to Anne and they have three children. He enjoys family time, reading, exercise, dancing, and the beach, and he is active in local organizations and community events.

paragraphs.text: We believe that a strong community leads to healthier, happier people. We support 39 schools in our area and partner with local organizations, churches, and programs to give back where our patients and staff live and work. We are proud to support Smiles Change Lives and other local initiatives that expand access to orthodontic care.

paragraphs.text: Your initial examination is a chance to meet our team, learn about orthodontic options, and get a complete oral exam with any necessary X-rays. Dr. Craig Hadgis will evaluate your bite and explain which treatments may fit your needs so you can make an informed choice.

We review your dental and medical history, create a customized treatment plan, and discuss financial details, insurance and payment plan options. Our team is here to answer questions and help you schedule follow-up visits at times that work for your family.

image.alt: Father with daughter at reception of dentist's surgery

paragraphs.text: The cost of orthodontic treatment varies with each plan. Our staff will review fees, payment options and available plans so you understand your choices before treatment begins.
We accept most major credit cards and insurance carriers, and our team will help verify benefits and file claims. Flexible payment plans are available to fit your budget.

To make your first visit efficient, please complete patient and insurance forms before you arrive. Use the links below to access online forms, request your free initial exam, or sign in to your patient account.

image.alt: Smiling patients of all ages at Lakeshore Orthodontics

paragraphs.text: Our smooth mini low-profile braces are the most common type, and are more comfortable today than ever. Made of high-grade stainless steel, metal braces straighten your teeth using metal brackets and archwires; patients may add colored elastics (rubber bands) for a more unique and colorful smile.

Self-ligating braces are made from the same materials as traditional braces but do not require elastics, which means fewer appointments and less friction on the tooth. They use a specialized clip in place of elastics to help the archwire guide teeth into place, reducing pressure on the tooth and requiring fewer adjustments.

Ceramic braces are made of clear materials and are therefore less visible on your teeth than metal braces, so they are used mainly on older teenagers and adult patients who have cosmetic concerns. Although visually less prominent, ceramic braces require more attention to oral hygiene and tend to be used more on upper front teeth than on lower teeth.

If you’ve been dreaming of a straighter smile without the hassle of metal braces, Invisalign<sup>®</sup> may be the ideal solution. Dr. Craig Hadgis offers expert Invisalign treatment designed to fit seamlessly into your lifestyle.

Invisalign uses a series of custom-made, clear aligning trays to gently shift your teeth into the proper position. Each aligner is uniquely crafted with controlled movement built into every stage of the process, typically changed every one to two weeks for a comfortable, convenient, and nearly invisible path to results.

image.alt: Close-up of Invisalign aligners being placed on teeth

paragraphs.text: We offer passive self-ligating Damon Smile braces, which use a slide mechanism to hold the archwire and reduce pressure on the teeth. Damon braces eliminate the need for elastic ties, use gentle low-friction forces for comfort, and are available in clear brackets for patients concerned with aesthetics.

Lingual braces are hidden behind the teeth and are therefore invisible when you smile. They are 100% customized to match the shape of your teeth and are created uniquely for each patient, making them a strong option for athletes, actors, musicians who play wind instruments, and adult professionals.

LightForce is a digital orthodontic system that provides customized, 3D-printed tooth-moving tools. The system combines proprietary treatment planning software with advanced 3D printing to create custom appliances that emphasize precise, fully custom mechanics, reduced treatment time, increased comfort, and fewer, shorter appointments.

After braces or aligner treatment, retainers preserve and stabilize your results by controlling changes in tooth position while gums, bone, and muscle adjust. We provide custom retainers—both removable and fixed—and guidance to help your new smile last.

image.alt: Beautiful young Asian woman smiling and holding orthodontic retainer

paragraphs.text: Find clear, practical answers to common questions about Invisalign<sup>®</sup> clear aligners, how they work, treatment expectations, and how to schedule your visit.

accordion.header: Will Invisalign treatment fit my lifestyle?

paragraphs.text: Yes. Invisalign aligners are removable, so you can take them out to eat, drink, brush and floss. They’re clear, so most people won’t notice them while you’re going about your day. You can remove them for special events when needed, and routine appointments are typically quick check-ins about every six weeks.

The Invisalign system uses a series of custom-made, nearly invisible aligners created from advanced 3D computer imaging. Each aligner is worn for about two weeks and moves your teeth gradually toward the planned final position. Your doctor maps out the full treatment plan so you can see the expected progression from start to finish.

accordion.header: What are the primary benefits of Invisalign treatment?

paragraphs.text: Invisalign<sup>®</sup> aligners are clear, making treatment discreet, and they’re removable so you can maintain normal eating and oral hygiene habits. That flexibility helps keep your teeth and gums healthy while you straighten your smile.

Your Invisalign treatment time is determined by your doctor and depends on your specific needs. Each aligner is typically worn for about two weeks before moving to the next in the series. For best results, wear aligners as instructed, remove them to eat and clean your teeth, and attend regular progress checks so we can adjust your plan when needed.
